broker_fee_detail: No broker fee under NYC FARE Act; listing by Smart NY Realty LLC
broker_message: Hi Joshua — interested in 650 Ocean Ave #6B. Before applying I'd like the DHCR rent registration history (I understand the building is rent-stabilized), the lease term, and full move-in costs. I also saw the building carries a large number of open HPD violations including heat/hot-water items — can you tell me which affect the 6th floor and the repair timeline? Given that, would the owner consider $1,750 or a free month? Thanks!
broker_questions: Is 6B rent-stabilized, and what is the registered legal rent (DHCR history)?
250 open HPD violations incl. 49 Class C — which affect the 6th floor / 6B, and repair timeline?
Were there heat/hot-water outages last winter? How fast does management respond?
Actual square footage and window exposure of 6B?
Full move-in cost and lease term (available 7/13)?
Any active litigation or bedbug treatment on my floor?
building_risk_rationale: 250 open HPD violations across 93 units = 2.68 violations/unit, roughly 3.3x the NYC average of 0.81. The mix is severe: 49 open Class C (immediately hazardous — heat/hot water/pests/mold/defective parts), 151 Class B, 50 Class A. Plus 14 litigation cases, 3 evictions, 2 bedbug reports in the past year. openigloo reviews independently describe flooding with no landlord help, a possible illegal basement apartment, ineffective heat, and having to call 311 to get anything fixed. Owner/manager Rush Realty (Isaac Stern) draws consistent neglect complaints. Clear High-risk; the 49 open Class C count is decisive.
commute_notes: Excellent transit: BMT Brighton (B/Q) at Church Av ~5 min. Q to Midtown ~35-40 min; B weekday express. Multiple Brighton stations within a 10-min walk.
concessions_to_ask: Reduce to $1,750 (studio comps run $1,799-$1,864 in cleaner buildings)
One month free or reduced deposit given building condition
Written commitment to correct Class C violations affecting the unit/floor before move-in
cons: 250 open HPD violations incl. 49 Class C; recurring heat complaints
Documented neglect, flooding, litigation and bedbug reports
Rent up 32% since 2020 on a stabilized unit — possibly above legal rent
leverage_notes: Strong tenant leverage: 250 open violations / 49 Class C, 14 litigations, and heat complaints are documented and serious; comparable studios nearby (2016 Albemarle $1,825, 483 Ocean Pkwy $1,864) sit in cleaner buildings for similar money. Rent jumped 32% since 2020 on a stabilized unit, so legal rent may cap what they can charge. Push hard on price and repairs; be ready to walk.
listed_by: Joshua Brick, Smart NY Realty LLC
noise_risks: Ocean Ave is a wide, busy through-street with steady traffic
plain_english: Location and transit are excellent, it's a no-fee rent-stabilized studio, and the price is in line with nearby studios. But the building is the problem: 250 open HPD violations including 49 immediately-hazardous Class C items, recurring heat complaints, 14 litigations, bedbug reports, and reviews about flooding and neglect make this high-risk. Just listed (not sitting). Only pursue if you get the DHCR legal-rent history, a price/free-month concession, and written commitments to fix the hazardous violations affecting your unit — otherwise walk. NOTE: this flips the screen's earlier 'low' read, which was based on a thin augrented figure; the deeper openigloo record shows the building is severely distressed.
pros: Outstanding transit — 5 min to B/Q on the Brighton line
No fee; confirmed rent-stabilized with renewal/increase protections

red_flags.how_to_verify: Pull HPD Online for the C-violation list; ask which apply to 6B/6th floor; ask about last winter's heat
red_flags.issue: Studio in a building with 250 open HPD violations incl. 49 Class C and recurring heat complaints
red_flags.why: Signals no heat/hot water, pests, mold, and unresponsive management — core livability risk

red_flags.how_to_verify: Request DHCR rent registration history before signing
red_flags.issue: Rent up ~32% from $1,400 (2020) to $1,850 on a likely rent-stabilized unit
red_flags.why: If stabilized, the legal rent may be well below $1,850 (preferential/improper increase); you could be overpaying vs. legal rent

red_flags.how_to_verify: Measure on tour; confirm window count/exposure for 6B
red_flags.issue: Studio, no sqft, no floor plan, only 5 photos; reviews mention cramped units
red_flags.why: Small studios in big prewar buildings can be tight/dark despite 'tons of windows'

red_flags.issue: Reviews cite flooding, a possible illegal basement apartment, and neglect
red_flags.why: Pattern of deferred maintenance and possible code issues

sources.label: openigloo 650 Ocean Ave (250 open violations, 49 Class C, litigation, reviews)
